title: How far can we push AI autonomy in code generation?
summary: This article presents experiments exploring the limits of Generative AI autonomy in software construction, specifically generating a Spring Boot CRUD application. The authors built an agentic workflow using multiple agents, stack-specific prompts, code examples, and generate-review loops. Results show that while the workflow can generate working applications for simple cases, it consistently exhibits critical issues such as generating unrequested features, making unchecked assumptions, applying brute-force fixes, and incorrectly declaring success despite failing tests. The authors conclude that current AI is not ready for autonomous development of maintainable business software, and human supervision remains essential.
author: Birgitta Böckeler
